learning_outcomes.description: locate basic brain structures and use anatomical terms
identify types of brain cells and their functions
specify how signals are transmitted through neurons
specify the consequences of damage to certain brain regions
define some key neurological syndromes (for example, agnosia, apraxia)
specify the distinctions between several types of key neurological syndromes
define and explain basic facts about how neurons, neurotransmitters and complex brain systems operate to produce normal and abnormal cognition, emotions, motivation and behaviour
demonstrate understanding of facts in tests and written reports
write an empirical report about a behavioural experiment, clearly defining theory, methods, data and results and inferences to be drawn from data and results
demonstrate, through active laboratory activities, a knowledge of some key perceptual functions
demonstrate, through active laboratory activities, an understanding of selected cognitive functions
understand and explain how both low-level abilities, such as perception and motor control, and higher-level abilities, such as inhibitory control and working memory, are organised in the brain
identify and explain the range of research methods used in neuroscience
consider how social and emotional aspects related to cognition are impacted by culture, including First Nations

assessments.task_description:
In class Quizzes

3 quizzes to be completed in tutorial classes.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
Content Tutorial 1 Week 2 Wed - Week 2 Fri

Content Tutorial 2 Week 4 Wed - Week 4 Fri

Content Tutorial 3 Week 6 Wed - Week 6 Fri

Content Tutorial 4 Week 7 Wed - Week 7 Fri

Content Tutorial 5 Week 8 Wed - Week 8 Fri

Content Tutorial 7 Week 11 Wed - Week 11 Fri

Content Tutorial 8 Week 12 Wed - Week 12 Fri
assessments.task_description:
Seven (of the 11) tutorials are content tutorials. A short answer worksheet (typically 2 or 3 questions each requiring 50-100 words) based on the tutorial activity is to be completed in the final 20 minutes of the tutorial and handed in at the end of the tutorial.

Each worksheet will be worth 5 marks and six of the seven will form the total worksheet mark (out of 35).

There are no options for deferral or late submission.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
